behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real `git` rename detection plus CLI categorization. The behavior token `Renamed files` depends on actual VCS status codes/similarity heuristics from a real subprocess; mocking git output can hide misclassification caused by real command flags/output formats. Integration is too low because the bug can appear only at the subprocess handoff. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML dependency in behavior text, so Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert the report increments `renamed` only (not `added`+`deleted`) for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior token `include binary files` implies real git diff/subprocess output and encoding edge cases; in-process stubs often remove the exact bytes/markers that trigger crashes. REAL-INFRA and MOCK-HIDE both push to System. Rejected higher levels: no LLM/model output quality or multi-agent sequence is mentioned.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary-related changes are counted in the structured stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior token `repository that has no commits yet` relies on real git repository state and real subprocess exit/error behavior (e.g., missing HEAD). Mocking can hide command failure-path differences. Integration brake (a) is yes: real subprocess/OS state required.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits, execute the CLI as a subprocess, and assert it returns a successful status plus an empty structured report instead of an exception/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Behavior token `actual change history of the repository` is end-to-end against real git history semantics across commits/status types. A fake history fixture can miss mismatches caused by real command output ordering/format and subprocess parsing. Rejected higher levels: no model judgment or agent pipeline appears in this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across multiple commits, run the CLI, and compare reported counts to independently computed expected counts from git commands for that repo.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Behavior token `staged or unstaged` is specifically about real git index vs working tree state transitions, which are OS+subprocess observable boundaries. Mocked repos often collapse these states and hide failures, so Integration is too low. Rejected higher levels: behavior has no AI/ML dependence and no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI for each case, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
